# Google Drive Connector - Full Documentation

# Google Drive Connector 1.1 Reference

> For the full documentation index, see: https://docs.mulesoft.com/llms.txt

## Configurations

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name for this configuration. Connectors reference the configuration with this name.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Config_Oauth2">Oauth2 Connection Provider</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>The connection types to provide to this configuration.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td><p>MINUTES</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>AUTO</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Name</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The identifier of this element used to reference it in other components</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Expiration Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#ExpirationPolicy">Expiration Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the minimum amount of time that a dynamic configuration instance can remain idle before Mule considers it eligible for expiration. This does not mean that the platform expires the instance at the exact moment that it becomes eligible. Mule purges the instances as appropriate.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Connection Types

### Oauth2 Connection Provider

Use OAuth 2.0 to delegate user authentication to the service hosting the user account. For more
information, refer to
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2) or
[Configuring OAuth 2.0 for Connectors on CloudHub](../../connectors/introduction/intro-config-oauth2-cloudhub).

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Default Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultHeader">Default Header</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Array of <a href="#DefaultQueryParam">Default Query Param</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for establishing connections to the remote service.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Connection Timeout field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Persistent Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>If false, each connection is closed after the first request is completed.</p></td><td><p>true</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Max Connections</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of outbound connections to keep open concurrently.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Timeout for how long a connection can remain idle before it is closed.</p></td><td><p>30</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Connection Idle Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the Connection Idle Timeout field.</p></td><td><p>SECONDS</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Proxy Config</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Proxy">Proxy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Reusable configuration element for outbound connections through a proxy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Stream Response</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether or not to stream received responses.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Buffer Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Space, in bytes, for the buffer where the HTTP response will be stored.</p></td><td><p>-1</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access_type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Indicates whether your application can refresh access tokens when the user is not present at the browser. Valid parameter values are online, which is the default value, and offline.</p></td><td><p>offline</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Prompt</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A space-delimited, case-sensitive list of prompts to present the user. If you donât specify this parameter, the user is prompted only the first time your project requests access.</p></td><td><p>consent</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Base URI</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Parameter base URI. Each instance or tenant gets its own base URI.</p></td><td><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/drive/v3</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Protocol</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>HTTP</p></li><li><p>HTTPS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Protocol to use for communication. Valid values are HTTP and HTTPS.</p></td><td><p>HTTP</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>TLS Configuration</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Tls">Tls</a></p></div></div></td><td></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#Reconnection">Reconnection</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>When the application is deployed, a connectivity test is performed on all connectors. If set to true, deployment fails if the test doesnât pass after exhausting the associated reconnection strategy.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth consumer key, as registered with the service provider.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Consumer Secret</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth consumer secret, as registered with the service provider</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorization Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs authorization endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/auth</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Access Token Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The service providerâs access token endpoint URL.</p></td><td><p><code>https://accounts.google.com/o/oauth2/token</code></p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scopes</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The OAuth scopes to request during the OAuth dance. If not provided, it defaults to the scopes in the annotation.</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.appdata</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.file</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.metadata</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.metadata.readonly</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.photos.readonly</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.readonly</code></p></li><li><p><code>https://www.googleapis.com/auth/drive.scripts</code></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Resource Owner Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The resource owner ID for each component to use if it is not otherwise referenced.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Before</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately before starting the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>After</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the flow to execute immediately after an access token is received.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Listener Config</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>References the HTTP configuration used to create the listener that receives the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Callback Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the access token callback endpoint.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Authorize Path</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Path of the local HTTP endpoint that triggers the OAuth dance.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>External Callback Url</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>URL that the OAuth provider uses to access the callback endpoint if the endpoint is behind a proxy or accessed through an indirect URL.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Object Store</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A reference to the object store to use to store each resource owner IDâs data. If not specified, Mule uses the default object store.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Sources

- [On New File Trigger](#OnNewFileTrigger)
- [On New Folder Trigger](#OnNewFolderTrigger)

## On New File Trigger

`<google-drive:on-new-file-trigger>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Datetime value as lower bound for a new file, for example 2020-06-30T12:41:53Z.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of field names to retrieve. By default, id, createdTime, kind, name, mimeType are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configurations](#Config)

## On New Folder Trigger

`<google-drive:on-new-folder-trigger>`

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Created</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Datetime value as lower bound for a new file, for example 2020-06-30T12:41:53Z.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Comma-separated list of field names to be retrieved. By default, id, createdTime, kind, name, mimeType are returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Primary Node Only</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether this source should only be executed on the primary node when running in a cluster.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Scheduling Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><p>scheduling-strategy</p></div></div></td><td><p>Configures the scheduler that triggers the polling</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The default is to use repeatable streams.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Redelivery Policy</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#RedeliveryPolicy">Redelivery Policy</a></p></div></div></td><td><p>Defines a policy for processing the redelivery of the same message.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Create changes watch

`<google-drive:create-changes-watch>`

Subscribes to changes for a user. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /changes/watch
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Items From All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include both My Drive and shared drive items in results.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of changes to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Team Drive Items</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use includeItemsFromAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. This should be set to the value of <code>nextPageToken</code> from the previous response or to the response from the getStartPageToken method.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use driveId instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Restrict To My Drive</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to restrict the results to changes inside the My Drive hierarchy. This omits changes to files such as those in the Application Data folder or shared files that have not been added to My Drive.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The shared drive from which changes are returned. If specified, the change IDs will be reflective of the shared drive; use the combined drive ID and change ID as an identifier.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Corpus Removals</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether changes should include the file resource if the file is still accessible by the user at the time of the request, even when a file was removed from the list of changes and there will be no further change entries for this file.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Removed</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include changes indicating that items have been removed from the list of changes, for example by deletion or loss of access.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>spaces</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of spaces to query within the user corpus. Supported values are <code>drive</code>, <code>appDataFolder</code>, and <code>photos</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create channels stop

`<google-drive:create-channels-stop>`

Stop watching resources through this channel. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/channels/stop endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Create drives

`<google-drive:create-drives>`

Creates a new shared drive. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /drives endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ID, such as a random UUID, that uniquely identifies this userâs request for idempotent creation of a shared drive. A repeated request by the same user with the same request ID will avoid creating duplicates by attempting to create the same shared drive. If the shared drive already exists a 409 error is returned.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND

## Create drives hide by drive id

`<google-drive:create-drives-hide-by-drive-id>`

Hides a shared drive from the default view. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/drives/{driveId}/hide endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the shared drive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. - GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create drives unhide by drive id

`<google-drive:create-drives-unhide-by-drive-id>`

Restores a shared drive to the default view. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/drives/{driveId}/unhide endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the shared drive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create files comments by file id

`<google-drive:create-files-comments-by-file-id>`

Creates a new comment on a file. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/files/{fileId}/comments endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. /files/{fileId}/comments/{commentId}/replies endpoint.
