## Create files copy by file id

`<google-drive:create-files-copy-by-file-id>`

Creates a copy of a file and applies any requested updates with patch semantics. Folders cannot be
copied. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /files/{fileId}/copy endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Default Visibility</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to ignore the domainâs default visibility settings for the created file. Domain administrators can choose to make all uploaded files visible to the domain by default; this parameter bypasses that behavior for the request. Permissions are still inherited from parent folders.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce Single Parent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Copying files into multiple folders is no longer supported. Use shortcuts instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keep Revision Forever</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to set the <code>keepForever</code> field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ocr Language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create files permissions by file id

`<google-drive:create-files-permissions-by-file-id>`

Creates a permission for a file or shared drive. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/files/{fileId}/permissions endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file or shared drive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Send Notification Email</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to send a notification email when sharing to users or groups. This defaults to true for users and groups, and is not allowed for other requests. It must not be disabled for ownership transfers.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce Single Parent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. See moveToNewOwnersRoot for details.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Email Message</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A plain text custom message to include in the notification email.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Transfer Ownership</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to transfer ownership to the specified user and downgrade the current owner to a writer. This parameter is required as an acknowledgment of the side effect.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, the requester will be granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Move To New Owners Root</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>This parameter only takes effect if the item is not in a shared drive and the request is attempting to transfer the ownership of the item. If set to true, the item is moved to the new ownerâs My Drive root folder and all prior parents are removed. If set to false, the parents are not changed.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create files watch by file id

`<google-drive:create-files-watch-by-file-id>`

Subscribes to changes to a file. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the
/files/{fileId}/watch endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acknowledge Abuse</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the user is acknowledging the risk of downloading known malware or other abusive files. This is only applicable when alt=media.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create media file

`<google-drive:create-media-file>`

Creates a new media file. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /files endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>Name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Default Visibility</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to ignore the domainâs default visibility settings for the created file. Domain administrators can choose to make all uploaded files visible to the domain by default; this parameter bypasses that behavior for the request. Permissions are still inherited from parent folders.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce Single Parent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Creating files in multiple folders is no longer supported.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keep Revision Forever</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to set the <code>keepForever</code> field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Content As Indexable Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use the uploaded content as indexable text.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ocr Language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME media type of the object being uploaded.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Length</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of bytes uploaded. Not required if you use chunked transfer encoding.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create multipart file

`<google-drive:create-multipart-file>`

Creates a new multipart file. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /files endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Default Visibility</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to ignore the domainâs default visibility settings for the created file. Domain administrators can choose to make all uploaded files visible to the domain by default; this parameter bypasses that behavior for the request. Permissions are still inherited from parent folders.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce Single Parent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Creating files in multiple folders is no longer supported.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keep Revision Forever</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to set the <code>keepForever</code> field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Content As Indexable Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use the uploaded content as indexable text.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Please use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ocr Language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME media type of the object being uploaded.</p></td><td><p>multipart/form-data; boundary= rc2_34b212</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Fields - Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content of the <code>fields</code> part.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content - Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content of the <code>content</code> part.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create resumable file - initial request

`<google-drive:create-resumable-file-initial-request>`

The initial request for creating a new resumable file. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to
the /files endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ignore Default Visibility</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to ignore the domainâs default visibility settings for the created file. Domain administrators can choose to make all uploaded files visible to the domain by default; this parameter bypasses that behavior for the request. Permissions are still inherited from parent folders.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce Single Parent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Creating files in multiple folders is no longer supported.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keep Revision Forever</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to set the <code>keepForever</code> field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Content As Indexable Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use the uploaded content as indexable text.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key. Your API key identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Please use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ocr Language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upload Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the file data, which is transferred in subsequent requests.</p></td><td><p>application/octet-stream</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upload Content Length</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of bytes of file data, which is transferred in subsequent requests.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Length</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of bytes in the body of this initial request.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Create resumable file - upload content

`<google-drive:create-resumable-file-upload-content>`

Uploads the content of a resumable file. Can also be used to resume an interrupted upload. This
operation makes an HTTP PUT request to the /files endpoint

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Location</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The resumable session URI.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the file data.</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Length</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of bytes in the current chunk.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Content Range</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Set to show which bytes in the file you upload.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to be used to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p><strong>x</strong></p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p><strong>Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td></tr><tr><td><p><strong>Attributes Type</strong></p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE == Create teamdrives `<google-drive:create-teamdrives>`

Deprecated use drives.create instead. This operation makes an HTTP POST request to the /teamdrives
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Request Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An ID, such as a random UUID, that uniquely identifies this userâs request for idempotent creation of a Team Drive. A repeated request by the same user with the same request ID will avoid creating duplicates by attempting to create the same Team Drive. If the Team Drive already exists a 409 error will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Delete drives by drive id

`<google-drive:delete-drives-by-drive-id>`

Permanently deletes a shared drive for which the user is an organizer. The shared drive cannot
contain any untrashed items. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the /drives/{driveId}
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the shared drive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. - GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete files by file id

`<google-drive:delete-files-by-file-id>`

Permanently deletes a file owned by the user without moving it to the trash. If the file belongs to
a shared drive the user must be an organizer on the parent. If the target is a folder, all
descendants owned by the user are also deleted. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/files/{fileId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce Single Parent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. If an item is not in a shared drive and its last parent is deleted but the item itself is not, the item will be placed under its ownerâs root.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete files comments by file ID comment id

`<google-drive:delete-files-comments-by-file-id-comment-id>`

Deletes a comment. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/files/{fileId}/comments/{commentId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Comment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the comment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

`<google-drive:delete-files-comments-replies-by-file-id-comment-id-reply-id>`

Deletes a reply. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/files/{fileId}/comments/{commentId}/replies/{replyId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Comment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the comment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reply Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the reply.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes.
