## Delete files permissions by file ID permission id

`<google-drive:delete-files-permissions-by-file-id-permission-id>`

Deletes a permission. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/files/{fileId}/permissions/{permissionId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file or shared drive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Permission Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the permission.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, the requester is granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Delete files revisions by file ID revision id

`<google-drive:delete-files-revisions-by-file-id-revision-id>`

Permanently deletes a file version. You can only delete revisions for files with binary content in
Google Drive, like images or videos. Revisions for other files, like Google Docs or Sheets, and the
last remaining file version canât be deleted. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to the
/files/{fileId}/revisions/{revisionId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revision Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the revision.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>String</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Delete files trash

`<google-drive:delete-files-trash>`

Permanently deletes all of the userâs trashed files. This operation makes an HTTP DELETE request to
the /files/trash endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce Single Parent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. If an item is not in a shared drive and its last parent is deleted but the item itself is not, the item will be placed under its ownerâs root.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Get about

`<google-drive:get-about>`

Gets information about the user, the userâs Drive, and system capabilities. This operation makes an
HTTP GET request to the /about endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Drive changes list

`<google-drive:get-changes>`

Lists the changes for a user or shared drive. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/changes endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Items From All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include both My Drive and shared drive items in results.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of changes to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Team Drive Items</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use includeItemsFromAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. Set to the value of <code>nextPageToken</code> from the previous response or to the response from the getStartPageToken method.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use driveId instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Restrict To My Drive</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to restrict the results to changes inside the My Drive hierarchy. This omits changes to files such as those in the Application Data folder or shared files which have not been added to My Drive.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The shared drive from which changes are returned. If specified, the change IDs will be reflective of the shared drive; use the combined drive ID and change ID as an identifier.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Corpus Removals</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include the file resource in changes if the file is still accessible by the user at the time of the request, even when a file was removed from the list of changes and there will be no further change entries for this file.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Removed</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include changes indicating that items have been removed from the list of changes, for example by deletion or loss of access.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>spaces</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of spaces to query within the user corpus. Supported values are <code>drive</code>, <code>appDataFolder</code> and <code>photos</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get changes start page token

`<google-drive:get-changes-start-page-token>`

Gets the starting pageToken for listing future changes. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to
the /changes/startPageToken endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use driveId instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the shared drive for which the starting pageToken for listing future changes from that shared drive is returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Drive drives list

`<google-drive:get-drives>`

Lists the userâs shared drives. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /drives endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of shared drives to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Page token for shared drives.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>q</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Query string for searching shared drives.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, all shared drives of the domain in which the requester is an administrator are returned.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get drives by drive id

`<google-drive:get-drives-by-drive-id>`

Gets a shared driveâs metadata by ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/drives/{driveId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the shared drive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, the requester is granted access if they are an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Drive files list

`<google-drive:get-files>`

Lists or searches files. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /files endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Items From All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include both My Drive and shared drive items in results.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>corpora</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Groupings of files to which the query applies. Supported groupings are: <code>user</code> (files created by, opened by, or shared directly with the user), <code>drive</code> (files in the specified shared drive as indicated by the <code>driveId</code>), <code>domain</code> (files shared to the userâs domain), and <code>allDrives</code> (A combination of <code>user</code> and <code>drive</code> for all drives where the user is a member). When able, use <code>user</code> or <code>drive</code>, instead of <code>allDrives</code>, for efficiency.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>corpus</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>DOMAIN</p></li><li><p>USER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>The source of files to list. Deprecated. Use <code>corpora</code> instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of files to return per page. Partial or empty result pages are possible even before the end of the files list has been reached.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Team Drive Items</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated use includeItemsFromAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. Set to the value of <code>nextPageToken</code> from the previous response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use driveId instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>q</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A query for filtering the file results. See the "Search for Files" guide for supported syntax.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Order By</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of sort keys. Valid keys are <code>createdTime</code>, <code>folder</code>, <code>modifiedByMeTime</code>, <code>modifiedTime</code>, <code>name</code>, <code>name_natural</code>, <code>quotaBytesUsed</code>, <code>recency</code>, <code>sharedWithMeTime</code>, <code>starred</code>, and <code>viewedByMeTime</code>. Each key sorts ascending by default, but may be reversed with the <code>desc</code> modifier. Example usage: ?orderBy=folder,modifiedTime desc,name. Please note that there is a current limitation for users with approximately one million files in which the requested sort order is ignored.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the shared drive to search.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>spaces</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of spaces to query within the corpus. Supported values are <code>drive</code>, <code>appDataFolder</code> and <code>photos</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get files by file id

`<google-drive:get-files-by-file-id>`

Gets a fileâs metadata or content by ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/files/{fileId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use Supports All Drives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acknowledge Abuse</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the user is acknowledging the risk of downloading known malware or other abusive files. This is only applicable when alt=media.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Drive comments list

`<google-drive:get-files-comments-by-file-id>`

Lists a fileâs comments. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /files/{fileId}/comments
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of comments to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. Set to the value of <code>nextPageToken</code> from the previous response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Deleted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include deleted comments. Deleted comments will not include their original content.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Start Modified Time</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The minimum value of <code>modifiedTime</code> for the result comments (RFC 3339 date-time).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get files comments by file ID comment id

`<google-drive:get-files-comments-by-file-id-comment-id>`

Gets a comment by ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/files/{fileId}/comments/{commentId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Comment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the comment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Deleted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to return deleted comments. Deleted comments will not include their original content.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
