- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Drive replies list

`<google-drive:get-files-comments-replies-by-file-id-comment-id>`

Lists a commentâs replies. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/files/{fileId}/comments/{commentId}/replies endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Comment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the comment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of replies to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. This should be set to the value of <code>nextPageToken</code> from the previous response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Deleted</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to include deleted replies. Deleted replies will not include their original content.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND

## Get files comments replies by file ID comment ID reply id

`<google-drive:get-files-comments-replies-by-file-id-comment-id-reply-id>`

Gets a reply by ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/files/{fileId}/comments/{commentId}/replies/{replyId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Comment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the comment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reply Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the reply.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Deleted replies will not include their original content.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get files export by file id

`<google-drive:get-files-export-by-file-id>`

Exports a Google Doc to the requested MIME type and returns the exported content. The exported
content is limited to 10MB. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /files/{fileId}/export
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The MIME type of the format requested for this export.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get files generate ids

`<google-drive:get-files-generate-ids>`

Generates a set of file IDs to provide in create or copy requests. This operation makes an HTTP GET
request to the /files/generateIds endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>count</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The number of IDs to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>space</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The space in which the IDs can be used to create new files. Supported values are <code>drive</code> and <code>appDataFolder</code>.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

## Drive permissions list

`<google-drive:get-files-permissions-by-file-id>`

Lists a fileâs or shared driveâs permissions. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/files/{fileId}/permissions endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file or shared drive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of permissions to return per page. When not set for files in a shared drive, at most 100 results will be returned. When not set for files that are not in a shared drive, the entire list will be returned.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. This should be set to the value of <code>nextPageToken</code> from the previous response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, the requester is granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Array of Any</p></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get files permissions by file ID permission id

`<google-drive:get-files-permissions-by-file-id-permission-id>`

Gets a permission by ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/files/{fileId}/permissions/{permissionId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Permission Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the permission.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, the requester is granted access if the file ID parameter refers to a shared drive and the requester is an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Drive revisions list

`<google-drive:get-files-revisions-by-file-id>`

Lists a fileâs revisions. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /files/{fileId}/revisions
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The maximum number of revisions to return per page.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The token for continuing a previous list request on the next page. Set to the value of <code>nextPageToken</code> from the previous response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Output Mime Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>The mime type of the payload that this operation outputs.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryIterable">Repeatable In Memory Iterable</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreIterable">Repeatable File Store Iterable</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-iterable</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get files revisions by file ID revision id

`<google-drive:get-files-revisions-by-file-id-revision-id>`

Gets a revisionâs metadata or content by ID. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/files/{fileId}/revisions/{revisionId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Revision Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the revision.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Acknowledge Abuse</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the user is acknowledging the risk of downloading known malware or other abusive files. This is only applicable when alt=media.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND

## Get teamdrives

`<google-drive:get-teamdrives>`

Deprecated. Use drives.list instead. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the /teamdrives
endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Size</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>Maximum number of Team Drives to return.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Page Token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Page token for Team Drives.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>q</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Query string for searching Team Drives.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, all Team Drives of the domain in which the requester is an administrator are returned.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td><p>#[null]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Get teamdrives by team drive id

`<google-drive:get-teamdrives-by-team-drive-id>`

Deprecated. Use drives.get instead. This operation makes an HTTP GET request to the
/teamdrives/{teamDriveId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Team Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the Team Drive</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, the requester is granted access if they are an administrator of the domain to which the Team Drive belongs.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. ## Patch drives by drive id

`<google-drive:patch-drives-by-drive-id>`

Updates the metadate for a shared drive. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the
/drives/{driveId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Drive Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the shared drive.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Domain Admin Access</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Issue the request as a domain administrator. If set to true, the requester is granted access if they are an administrator of the domain to which the shared drive belongs.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>Content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. ## Patch files by file id

`<google-drive:patch-files-by-file-id>`

Updates a fileâs metadata and content. This method supports patch semantics. This operation makes an
HTTP PATCH request to the /files/{fileId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports All Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether the requesting application supports both My Drives and shared drives.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Enforce Single Parent</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Adding files to multiple folders is no longer supported. Use shortcuts instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Keep Revision Forever</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to set the <code>keepForever</code> field in the new head revision. This is only applicable to files with binary content in Google Drive. Only 200 revisions for the file can be kept forever. If the limit is reached, try deleting pinned revisions.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Use Content As Indexable Text</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Whether to use the uploaded content as indexable text.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Include Permissions For View</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Specifies which additional viewâs permissions to include in the response. Only <code>published</code> is supported.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Ocr Language</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A language hint for OCR processing during image import (ISO 639-1 code).</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Supports Team Drives</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated use supportsAllDrives instead.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Upload Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>The type of upload request to the /upload URI. If you are uploading data using an /upload URI, this field is required. If you are creating a metadata-only file, this field is not required. Additionally, this field is not shown in the <strong>Try this API</strong> widget because the widget doesnât support data uploads. Acceptable values are:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>media - Simple upload. Upload the media only, without any metadata.</p></li><li><p>multipart - Multipart upload. Upload both the media and its metadata, in a single request.</p></li><li><p>resumable - Resumable upload. Upload the file in a resumable way, using a series of at least two requests where the first request includes the metadata.</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Remove Parents</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of parent IDs to remove.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Add Parents</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>A comma-separated list of parent IDs to add.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

### For Configurations

- [Configurations](#Config)

### Throws

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVICE_UNAVAILABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TIMEOUT
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:TOO_MANY_REQUESTS
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNAUTHORIZED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:UNSUPPORTED_MEDIA_TYPE

## Patch files comments by file ID comment id

`<google-drive:patch-files-comments-by-file-id-comment-id>`

Updates a comment with patch semantics. This operation makes an HTTP PATCH request to the
/files/{fileId}/comments/{commentId} endpoint.

### Parameters

<table><colgroup><col> <col> <col> <col> <col></colgroup><thead><tr><th>Name</th><th>Type</th><th>Description</th><th>Default Value</th><th>Required</th></tr></thead><tbody><tr><td><p>Configuration</p></td><td><p>String</p></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>File Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the file.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Comment Id</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>ID of the comment.</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Pretty Print</p></td><td><div><div><p>Boolean</p></div></div></td><td><p>Returns the response with indentations and line breaks.</p></td><td><p>false</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Quota User</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>An opaque string that represents a user for quota purposes. Must not exceed 40 characters.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>key</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>API key that identifies your project and provides you with API access, quota, and reports. Required unless you provide an OAuth 2.0 token.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>User Ip</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Deprecated. Use quotaUser instead.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Oauth token</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>OAuth 2.0 token for the current user.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>alt</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>JSON</p></li><li><p>MEDIA</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Data format for the response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>fields</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Selector specifying which fields to include in a partial response.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Body</p></td><td><div><div><p>Any</p></div></div></td><td><p>The content to use.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Config Ref</p></td><td><div><div><p>ConfigurationProvider</p></div></div></td><td><p>The name of the configuration to use to execute this component</p></td><td></td><td><p>x</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#RepeatableInMemoryStream">Repeatable In Memory Stream</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#RepeatableFileStoreStream">Repeatable File Store Stream</a></p></li><li><p>non-repeatable-stream</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Configures how Mule processes streams. Repeatable streams are the default behavior.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Query Parameters</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom query parameters to include in the request. The specified query parameters are merged with the default query parameters that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Custom Headers</p></td><td><div><div><p>Object</p></div></div></td><td><p>Custom headers to include in the request. The specified custom headers are merged with the default headers that are specified in the configuration.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout</p></td><td><div><div><p>Number</p></div></div></td><td><p>The timeout for requests to the remote service.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Response Timeout Unit</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>NANOSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MICROSECONDS</p></li><li><p>MILLISECONDS</p></li><li><p>SECONDS</p></li><li><p>MINUTES</p></li><li><p>HOURS</p></li><li><p>DAYS</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>Time unit for the <strong>Response Timeout</strong> field.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Streaming Type</p></td><td><div><div><p>Enumeration, one of:</p></div><div><ul><li><p>AUTO</p></li><li><p>ALWAYS</p></li><li><p>NEVER</p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><div><div><p>Specifies how the connector streams request content to the remote service:</p></div><div><ul><li><p><code>AUTO</code> (default)</p><div><p>Connector automatically uses the best streaming strategy based on the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>ALWAYS</code></p><div><p>Connector always streams the request content.</p></div></li><li><p><code>NEVER</code></p><div><p>Connector does not stream the request content.</p></div></li></ul></div></div></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Variable</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Name of the variable that stores the operationâs output.</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Target Value</p></td><td><div><div><p>String</p></div></div></td><td><p>Expression that evaluates the operationâs output. The outcome of the expression is stored in the <strong>Target Variable</strong>.</p></td><td><p>#[payload]</p></td><td></td></tr><tr><td><p>Reconnection Strategy</p></td><td><div><div><ul><li><p><a href="#Reconnect">Reconnect</a></p></li><li><p><a href="#ReconnectForever">Reconnect Forever</a></p></li></ul></div></div></td><td><p>A retry strategy in case of connectivity errors</p></td><td></td><td></td></tr></tbody></table>

### Output

<table><colgroup><col> <col></colgroup><tbody><tr><td><p>Type</p></td><td><p>Any</p></td></tr><tr><td><p>Attributes Type</p></td><td><div><div><p><a href="#HttpResponseAttributes">Http Response Attributes</a></p></div></div></td></tr></tbody></table>

- [Configurations](#Config)

- GOOGLE-DRIVE:BAD_REQUEST
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CLIENT_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:CONNECTIVITY
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:INTERNAL_SERVER_ERROR
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_ACCEPTABLE
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:NOT_FOUND
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:RETRY_EXHAUSTED
- GOOGLE-DRIVE:SERVER_ERROR
